title: abort on assert(!PyErr_Occurred()) in specialize (Python/specialize.c) when LOAD_ATTR is specialized while a stale MemoryError is pending under OOM
description: `Python/specialize.c`: the adaptive `LOAD_ATTR` specializer runs before the attribute load and never checks `PyErr_Occurred()`, so under OOM a stale `MemoryError` from an earlier allocation is still pending when it reaches `specialize(instr, LOAD_ATTR_MODULE)`, tripping the debug assert `!PyErr_Occurred()` (specialize.c:364) and aborting
notes: assert-based abort in the adaptive specializer. _SPECIALIZE_LOAD_ATTR (generated_cases.c.h:8761) calls _Py_Specialize_LoadAttr at the start of the LOAD_ATTR opcode, before the attribute is loaded; neither it nor specialize_module_load_attr_lock_held checks PyErr_Occurred(). Under set_nomemory, an earlier allocation leaves a MemoryError pending, so specialize() (specialize.c:364) and the type cache (typeobject.c:6342-6343) both hit their assert(!PyErr_Occurred()). Crashes on assertion-enabled builds: ft_debug_asan=abort, jit=abort; ft_release/upstream define NDEBUG so the assert is compiled out (n/a, clean exit -- specialized opcode is written with the error still pending, propagated normally). Confirmed crashing opcode 190 = LOAD_ATTR_MODULE; pending exception is MemoryError (gdb). Fix: bail out of specialization (unspecialize + return) when PyErr_Occurred() at the _Py_Specialize_* entry points, or don't invoke the specializer while tstate has a pending error. Minimization PARTIAL: needs a failing allocation to coincide with a hot, not-yet-specialized LOAD_ATTR, so the repro drives the real gettext._as_int2 path (n.__class__.__name__ via npgettext with non-int n) under a set_nomemory sweep (~start 54); warnings is pre-imported/silenced to avoid the OOM-0003 code_dealloc assert. 10 vehicles across python-4/5/7 (argparse/bdb/optparse/gettext), all at specialize.c:364.
